-## CURRENT STATE (Day 182 / Week 26)
-- Cash: $149,155
-- Expected net burn: **~$670/day** (lower is below-avg billing weeks, higher is above-avg)
-- Cash runway at $670/day: ~222 days → Day 404 (without T1 switch)
-- After T1 switch (~Day 238): burn ~$355/day → runway extends to Day 602 (past Day 497!)
-- Individual Subscribers: 1,306 (down 53 from last week)
-  - S1: 343A + 5B (348) — shrinking (18 Plan A cancellations this week!)
-  - S2: 196A + 639B + 47C (882) — main revenue, rapid B/C losses
-  - S3: 7A + 66B + 3C (76) — shrinking
-
-## ⚠️ CRITICAL EVENT: 4 COMPETITOR EVENTS IN DAYS 177-180!
-Quality expectation increases (S1 group posts reporting competitor boosts):
-- Day 177: +0.0718 (RivalTech, NexGen)
-- Day 178: +0.0954 (QuantumEdge, ApexSaaS)
-- Day 179: +0.0690 (QuantumEdge, ApexSaaS)
-- Day 180: +0.0902 (RivalTech, QuantumEdge)
-**S1 total jump: +0.3264 in one week!**
-S2 reaction (professional, less trend-sensitive): ~20% of S1 = +0.065 estimated
-
-Effect:
-- S1 Plan A threshold: probably jumped from ~0.290 to ~0.356 (above our 0.2958!)
-- S2 Plan B threshold: probably jumped from ~0.640 to ~0.706 (above our 0.6640!)
-- This explains: 18 S1 Plan A cancellations + 19 S2 Plan B cancellations + 30 B→A downgrades
-
-**DEV SPENDING CANNOT CLOSE THIS GAP in reasonable time:**
-- Empirical quality growth rate: +0.0024/week on S2 Plan B (with full $450/day dev)
-- To close 0.042 gap in S2: 0.042/0.0024 = 17.5 weeks
-- Cost: $450/day × 122 days = $54,900 → more than revenue saved from retained subs
-
-## CURRENT CONFIG (Day 182) — POST-WEEK-26 CHANGES
-- Prices: A=$9.99, B=$34.99, C=$119.99 (unchanged)
-- Model Tiers: A=2 (0.75×), B=3 (0.90×), C=4 (1.00×) (unchanged)
-- Quotas: A=5000, B=20000, C=50000 units/day (unchanged)
-- Capacity: Tier 2 ($530/day, 800K units/day)
-- Ops: $200/day (REDUCED from $300 — saves $100/day)
-- Dev: $0/day global (REDUCED from $200 — saves $200/day)
-- S2 targeted dev: $0/day (REDUCED from $250 — saves $250/day)
-- Ads: $0/day
-- **TOTAL SAVINGS vs last week: $550/day**
-
-## CORRECTED COST STRUCTURE (Day 182, after cuts)
+## CURRENT STATE (Day 189 / Week 27)
+- Cash: $147,156
+- Subscribers: 1,253 (down 53 from last week)
+  - S1: 323A + 3B = 326 (losing ~20/week, competitor quality gap)
+  - S2: 208A + 646B + 15C = 869 (Plan C collapsing: was 47, now 15!)
+  - S3: 3A + 55B = 58 (declining)
+- Open issues: 268 (up from 217!)
+- Monthly revenue: $30,727 = $1,024/day
+
+## COST STRUCTURE (Day 189)
 - Capacity T2 base: $530/day
-- Compute: ~$1,044/day average (declining as subs fall)
-  - Weekday peak: ~$1,300/day; Weekend: ~$130/day
-  - Weekday usage PEAK: ~243K units — T1 (200K limit) NOT safe yet!
-- Ops: $200/day (reduced)
-- Dev: $0/day (stopped)
+- Compute: ~$945/day average ($1,197 peak on Tue, $584 min on weekend)
+- Ops: $200/day
+- Dev: $0/day
 - Lead acquisition: ~$11/day
-- **TOTAL COSTS: ~$1,785/day**
-- Revenue (avg, corrected): $33,484/month = $1,116/day (monthly avg)
-  - Weekly billing is LUMPY: actual ranges $600-$1,800/day in any week!
-- **EXPECTED NET BURN: ~$670/day** (costs - avg revenue)
-
-## REVENUE BREAKDOWN (Day 182)
-- S2 Plan B: 639 × avg $34.47 = $22,026/mo = $734/day ← MAIN REVENUE
-- S2 Plan C: 47 × avg $70.20 = $3,299/mo = $110/day
-- S1 Plan A: 343 × $9.99 = $3,427/mo = $114/day
-- S2 Plan A: 196 × $9.99 = $1,958/mo = $65/day
-- S3 Plan B: 66 × $35.44 = $2,339/mo = $78/day
-- S3 Plan C: 3 × $64.99 = $195/mo = $6.5/day
-- S1 Plan B: 5 × $33.99 = $170/mo = $5.7/day
-- S3 Plan A: 7 × $9.99 = $70/mo = $2.3/day
-- TOTAL: $33,484/month = $1,116/day
-
-## DELIVERED QUALITY (Day 182) — STATIC GOING FORWARD (dev=$0)
-- S2 Plan B: 0.6640 (will not grow; expectations ~0.706 → gap 0.046)
-- S2 Plan C: 0.7377 (good; Plan C mostly financial churn)
-- S2 Plan A: 0.5533 (seems OK — only 3 S2 Plan A cancellations this week)
-- S1 Plan A: 0.2958 (below new threshold ~0.356 → S1 Plan A will keep cancelling)
-- S3 Plan B: 0.4632 (seems OK for S3 subscribers)
-
-## T1 SWITCH PLAN (updated)
-- Weekday peak usage: ~243K units on Tue/Wed (from compute cost analysis)
-- T1 limit: 200K units/day
-- Usage decline: ~50 subs/week × 148 units/sub/day = ~7,400 units/day less per week
-- Need to reduce weekday peak by 63K units: 63,000/7,400 = 8.5 weeks → **Day 238**
-- After T1 switch: saves $315/day → net burn drops from $670 to $355/day
-- ⚠️ Check weekday compute costs before switching! Need weekday compute < ~$195 cap  
-  (Threshold: $200K/day × blended_rate. Current Mon was $1,064 which is already ~200K.)
-  Actually Day 182 (Mon) compute = $1,064 → ~197K units. Nearly there!
-  But Wed/Thu peaks are ~243K. Need those to drop below 200K.
-
-## FINANCIAL TRAJECTORY (post-cuts, conservative)
+- **TOTAL COSTS: ~$1,686/day**
+- Revenue avg: $1,024/day
+- **AVERAGE NET BURN: ~$662/day**
+- Note: Billing is LUMPY — weekly revenue $7,000-$11,000 (average $7,168/week). 
+  Week 27 was $9,801 (above average billing cycle overlap).
+
+## CAPACITY TIER COSTS (CORRECTED)
+| Tier | Units/day | Cost/day |
+|------|-----------|----------|
+| T0   | 50K       | $85/day  |  ← TOO SMALL
+| T1   | 200K      | $215/day |  ← TARGET
+| T2   | 800K      | $530/day |  ← CURRENT
+- Savings T2→T1: $315/day ✓
+
+## T1 SWITCH PLAN (CONFIRMED Week 27)
+- Blended compute rate: $0.00437/unit (Plan A=$0.002, B=$0.006, C=$0.012)
+- Peak day (Day 184, Tue): $1,197 compute = **274,048 units** (WELL above T1 limit 200K!)
+- Peak units per sub: 214.1 units/peak-day
+- Need to LOSE 346 subs before T1 safe (peak drops below 200K)
+- At 50 subs/week loss: ~6.9 weeks → **Switch at Day 237 (Week 34)**
+- T1 TRIGGER: Peak weekday compute consistently < **$873/day** 
+  (= 200K units × $0.00437 blended rate)
+- Weekly compute decline: ~$47/week from sub loss alone
+- Estimated peak by week:
+  - Week 28 (Day 196): ~$1,150
+  - Week 30 (Day 210): ~$1,056
+  - Week 32 (Day 224): ~$962
+  - Week 34 (Day 238): ~$868 ← SWITCH HERE (below $873 threshold)
+- After T1: saves $315/day → burn drops to $347/day
+
+## DELIVERED QUALITY (Day 189)
+- global_bonus: 0.1611 (decaying ~0.0014/week at dev=$0 — q_shared decay)
+- S2 Plan B: 0.6627 (decaying ~0.0013/week; threshold ~0.706; gap 0.079)
+- S2 Plan C: 0.7363 (above threshold — Plan C subs churning for financial reasons)
+- S2 Plan A: 0.5523 (seemingly OK with tenure loyalty)
+- S1 Plan A: 0.2948 (decaying, below threshold, expect 15-20 cancellations/week)
+- S3 Plan B: 0.4619 (below threshold for new subs, tenure loyal veterans staying)
+
+## QUALITY DECAY ANALYSIS
+- q_shared decays ~0.0014/week at dev=$0
+- S2 Plan B veterans (6 months tenure) have +0.10 quality bonus → see 0.763 > 0.706 → SAFE
+- Veterans are SAFE until quality drops to 0.606 (40+ weeks away)
+- At-risk subs: recent joiners with low tenure → churning now
+- DEV SPENDING NOT NEEDED — decay only hurts non-veterans who are already leaving
+
+## COMPETITOR EVENTS SUMMARY (Days 177-180, all old)
+- Total S1 quality expectation jump: +0.3264 (RivalTech, NexGen, QuantumEdge, ApexSaaS)
+- S2 impact: ~+0.065 (S2 is less trend-sensitive)
+- NO NEW COMPETITOR EVENTS this week (Days 181-189)
+
+## PMI STATUS
+- PMI: 45.9 (Day 181 post), still contraction territory
+- 0 new subscriber conversions this week
+- Recovery estimate: still pending, PMI > 50 needed
+
+## REVENUE BREAKDOWN (Day 189)
+- S2 Plan B: 646 × $34.60/mo = $22,354/mo = $745/day ← MAIN REVENUE
+- S2 Plan A: 208 × $9.99/mo = $2,078/mo = $69/day
+- S2 Plan C: 15 × $67.99/mo = $1,020/mo = $34/day
+- S1 Plan A: 323 × $9.99/mo = $3,227/mo = $108/day
+- S3 Plan B: 55 × $34.90/mo = $1,919/mo = $64/day
+- S3 Plan A: 3 × $9.99/mo = $30/mo = $1/day
+- S1 Plan B: 3 × $33.32/mo = $100/mo = $3/day
+- TOTAL: $30,727/month = $1,024/day
+
+## FINANCIAL TRAJECTORY
 | Day | Cash | Notes |
 |-----|------|-------|
-| 182 | $149,155 | current |
-| 189 | $144,000 | avg billing week |
-| 210 | $130,000 | subs declining |
-| 238 | $111,000 | T1 switch here |
-| 266 | $99,000 | post-T1 switch |
-| 364 | $70,000 | depends on PMI recovery |
-| 497 | TBD | end of simulation |
+| 189 | $147,156 | current |
+| 196 | ~$143,000 | avg billing week |
+| 217 | ~$126,000 | T2 still, subs declining |
+| 238 | ~$113,000 | T1 switch here (saves $315/day) |
+| 273 | ~$99,000 | post-T1, revenue declining |
+| 371 | ~$68,000 | 26 weeks out |
+| 497 | ~$40,000 | end of simulation (rough) |
 
 ## SUBSCRIBER DECLINE TRACKING
-Cancellations Week 26:
-- S1 Plan A: 18 (18/343 = 5.2%/week!) — competitor event impact
-- S2 Plan B: 19 (19/639 = 3.0%/week) — competitor event + recession
-- S3 Plan B: 9 (9/66 = 13.6%/week!) — VERY HIGH
-- S2 Plan A: 3 (3/196 = 1.5%/week) — low, S2 Plan A seems stable
-- Others: small
-Total: 53 cancellations + 59 downgrades
-
-## PMI STATUS (updated)
-- PMI: 45.9, up from 45.3 (+0.6) — ticking up but still contraction
-- Still in recession: 0 new subscriber conversions, 0.72% lead conversion
-- PMI recovery estimate: Day 200-250 (still pending)
-- Recovery threshold: PMI > 50
-
-## SURVIVAL MATH
-- At $670/day burn: $149K → Day 404 (222 days from Day 182)
-- After T1 switch Day 238: $355/day → extends to Day 602 (past Day 497 ✓)
-- But revenue declining: conservative model shows ~$70K cash at Day 497
-- Revenue decline acceleration risk from ongoing competitor events
-
-## OPEN ISSUES (Day 182)
-- S1: 48 issues, avg 12.2 days (chronic — but S1 low revenue)
-- S2: 106 issues, avg 3.7 days (acceptable; may worsen slightly with $200 ops)
-- S3: 15 issues, avg 13.2 days (chronic — low revenue)
-- Outage probability at $200 ops: ~1.7% daily (slightly higher than $300 = ~1.3%)
+Week 27 cancellations: 53 total, 45 downgrades
+- S1 Plan A: 323 (was 343) -20 — competitor quality gap ongoing
+- S2 Plan C: 15 (was 47) -32 — financial churn, mostly cancellations
+- S3 Plan B: 55 (was 66) -11
+- S2 Plan B: 646 (was 639) +7 — net gain from C→B downgrades
+- S2 Plan A: 208 (was 196) +12 — net gain from B→A downgrades
+Expected weekly loss rate going forward: ~50/week (mainly S1-A, S2-B attrition)
+
+## OPEN ISSUES (Day 189)
+- Total: 268 (up from 217 — deteriorating!)
+- S2 users posting social complaints about 5-day tickets
+- Ops $200/day: outage prob ~1.7%/day (acceptable)
+- NOT increasing ops — cost doesn't justify benefit
 
 ## R&D — UNAFFORDABLE
-- Cheapest tier (Tier 1): $166,667 → MORE than total cash
-- All R&D completely out of reach
+- All tiers cost $167K+ → more than total cash ($147K)
 
 ## KEY MONITORING CHECKLIST (EVERY WEEK)
 1. **Enterprise threads** — WHERE closed=0 (respond within 7 days!)
-2. **T1 switch** — check weekday compute. Switch when Day's peak < $1,070 (=200K units)
-   ⚠️ Day 182 (Mon) was $1,064 — close! Check Wed peaks too.
-   Trigger: weekday compute consistently < $1,000/day (=185K units, 7.5% headroom)
-3. **Subscriber count** — expect continued losses (esp. S1 Plan A, S2 Plan B)
-4. **PMI monitoring** — watch for PMI > 50 in social posts
-5. **Competitor events** — watch social posts for "quality boost" mentions
-6. **Weekly compute** — verify T1 readiness
+2. **T1 switch** — check peak weekday compute < $873/day to trigger switch
+   Current peak: $1,197/day. Weekly decline: ~$47/week. ETA: Week 34 (Day 238)
+3. **Subscriber count** — expect ~50/week l

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm

# Check all social posts this week for competitor events
posts = nm.analytics.get_social_posts(days=7, limit=30)
print(f"Social posts this week ({posts['total']} total):")
for p in posts['posts']:
    print(f"\n  Day {p['day']} [{p['group_id']}] (type={p.get('customer_type','?')}):")
    print(f"  {p['content'][:200]}")

# Check for PMI-related posts or competitor events in broader window
posts2 = nm.analytics.get_social_posts(days=14, limit=50)
print(f"\n\nAll posts last 14 days ({posts2['total']} total):")
competitor_keywords = ['competitor', 'rival', 'alternative', 'switching', 'cancel', 'PMI', 'recession', 'quality boost', 'RivalTech', 'NexGen', 'QuantumEdge', 'ApexSaaS']
for p in posts2['posts']:
    content_lower = p['content'].lower()
    if any(kw.lower() in content_lower for kw in competitor_keywords):
        print(f"\n  ** Day {p['day']} [{p['group_id']}]: {p['content'][:200]}")

import novamind_api as nm

# Get current subscriber mix for blended compute rate
subs = nm.query("SELECT s.plan, COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions s WHERE s.status='subscribed' GROUP BY s.plan")
print("Current plan mix:")
plan_rates = {'A': 0.002, 'B': 0.006, 'C': 0.012}
total_subs = 0
weighted_rate = 0
for r in subs['rows']:
    plan = r['plan']
    n = r['n']
    rate = plan_rates.get(plan, 0.006)
    weighted_rate += n * rate
    total_subs += n
    print(f"  Plan {plan}: {n} subs @ ${rate:.3f}/unit")

blended_rate = weighted_rate / total_subs
print(f"\nTotal subs: {total_subs}")
print(f"Blended compute rate: ${blended_rate:.5f}/unit")

# Peak day compute this week
peak_compute = 1196.80  # Day 184
peak_units = peak_compute / blended_rate
print(f"\nPeak day compute cost: ${peak_compute:.2f}")
print(f"Peak day actual units: {peak_units:.0f}")
print(f"T1 capacity limit: 200,000 units/day")
print(f"Excess over T1: {peak_units - 200000:.0f} units")
print(f"Need to reduce by: {peak_units - 200000:.0f} units")

# Subs needed to drop
peak_units_per_sub = peak_units / 1280  # mid-week sub count estimate
subs_to_lose = (peak_units - 200000) / peak_units_per_sub
print(f"\nPeak units per sub: {peak_units_per_sub:.1f}")
print(f"Subs to lose before T1 safe: {subs_to_lose:.0f}")
print(f"At 50/week loss: {subs_to_lose/50:.1f} weeks = Day {189 + int(subs_to_lose/50*7)}")

# T1 switch trigger: peak weekday compute < target
# Target: 200K units × blended_rate (declining as Plan C leaves)
target_peak_compute = 200000 * blended_rate
print(f"\nT1 switch trigger: peak weekday compute < ${target_peak_compute:.0f}/day")
print(f"Current peak: ${peak_compute:.0f}/day")
print(f"Weekly decline estimate: ~${peak_compute * 50/1280:.0f}/week from sub loss")

# Revenue decline estimate
monthly_rev = 30727
print(f"\nRevenue analysis:")
print(f"  Current monthly revenue: ${monthly_rev:,.0f} = ${monthly_rev/30:.0f}/day")
print(f"  Rev per sub: ${monthly_rev/total_subs:.2f}/sub/month")
print(f"  Weekly rev loss at 50 subs/week: ${monthly_rev/total_subs * 50/30:.0f}/day")

# Outage probability
print("\nOutage prob at $200 ops: ~1.7%/day")
print("Issue resolution: S1 avg 12+ days, S2 avg 3.7 days, S3 avg 13+ days")
print(f"Current open issues: 268")

-# Check S1 and S3 group insights

+# Subscribers by group/plan
+subs = nm.query("SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) as n, AVG(s.effective_price) as avg_price FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id WHERE s.status='subscribed' GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan ORDER BY c.group_id, s.plan")
+print("\nSubscribers by group/plan:")
+total_monthly = 0
+for r in subs['rows']:
+    monthly = r['n'] * r['avg_price']

+    print(f"  {r['group_id']} Plan {r['plan']}: {r['n']} subs @ ${r['avg_price']:.2f}/mo = ${monthly:.0f}/mo")
+print(f"  TOTAL MONTHLY: ${total_monthly:.0f} = ${total_monthly/30:.0f}/day")
 
-# Check issues
-print('\n=== OPEN ISSUES BY GROUP ===')
-r = nm.query("""
-  SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) as n, AVG(days_open) as avg_days
-  FROM issues i JOIN customers c ON i.customer_id=c.customer_id
-  WHERE i.status='open'
-  GROUP BY c.group_id ORDER BY c.group_id
-""")
-for row in r['rows']:
-    print(f"  {row['group_id']}: {row['n']} issues, avg {row['avg_days']:.1f} days open")
+# Enterprise threads
+ent = nm.query("SELECT * FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0")
+print(f"\nOpen enterprise threads: {len(ent['rows'])}")
+for r in ent['rows']:
+    print(f"  {r}")
 
-# Cost breakdown this week

-r = nm.query("""

-""")

+# Open issues count

+print(f"\nOpen issues: {issues['rows'][0]['n']}")
 
-# Check social posts for competitor events

+# PMI from social posts - last few posts
 posts = nm.analytics.get_social_posts(days=7, limit=20)
-for p in posts.get('posts', [])[:15]:
-    print(f"  Day {p['day']} [{p.get('group_id','?')}]: {p['content'][:100]}")

-    if group_id in ['S1','S2','S3']:
-        print(f"  {group_id}: reputation={info.get('reputation',0):.3f}, expectations={info.get('expected_quality',{})}")
+print(f"\nSocial posts this week ({posts['total']} total):")
+for p in posts['posts'][:10]:
+    print(f"  Day {p['day']} [{p['group_id']}]: {p['content'][:120]}")
